property_scores.justification: Precise 'Education' match with strong direct evidence from 80 schools, 6 ELCs, and homepage confirmation.
Catholic systemic K-12 (faith-based, government-funded, non-selective) precisely defined with diocesan and funding model evidence.
Clear end-service-provider with 43,000 direct student delivery; EdTech buyer/integrator role documented.
Mature sector confirmed with stable regulatory frameworks, 5.7% incremental growth, and established funding models.
Grant-funded plus modest fees precisely documented with Commonwealth/NSW recurrent and CSNSW 2024-2029 arrangement details.
Direct human-delivered classroom education plus School of Now blended supplement explicitly described with delivery mechanisms.
Fixed-cost-heavy confirmed by 4,500 staff and 80+ facilities with active capital works program evidence.
Four structural moats documented (geographic catchment, religious loyalty, government funding ties, accreditation barriers) with specific evidence.
Four critical dependencies precisely identified (government funding, NESA compliance, diocese governance, teacher supply) with detailed evidence.
Enterprise band (1000+) confirmed by explicit 4,500 staff figure consistent with 80-campus footprint.
Enterprise ($500M+) inferred from 16% NSW Catholic enrolment share and $3.65B sector funding; not publicly audited per-entity.
Government-grant-funded nonprofit (company limited by guarantee 2023) with CSNSW 2024-2029 funding certainty confirmed.
Asset-heavy confirmed by 80+ school campuses, 6 ELC sites, and active Westmead primary school capital works (opening 2026).
Fragmented Catholic education market reasonable for sector; diocese boundary creates local monopoly without broader competitive analysis.
Market leader confirmed as sole Catholic diocesan system in region serving one-in-four Western Sydney students.
Community/mission-driven enrolment precisely described (Education Mass, Bishop's Awards, LIFTED forum) with no outbound sales.
Diversified customer base confirmed by 43,000 students across 80 schools; government funding concentration separately identified.
Mid-market/accessible supported by 'modest fees' descriptor; lacks specific pricing tiers or fee range quantification.
Professional management with CEO Jack de Groot, 9 EGMs, and board governance under Elizabeth Crouch AM documented precisely.
Hub-and-spoke topology precisely identified (central system office + 80 semi-autonomous principals); standard for diocesan systems.
Multi-site distributed model documented for 80+ Western Sydney campuses; inherently on-campus education delivery.
Domain-expert-heavy (NESA-accredited teachers) confirmed with Teacher Scholarship Program and ICT traineeship pipeline evidence.
Transitional classification supported by MacBooks/Chromebooks, School of Now blended platform; lacks specific technical architecture details.
N/A appropriately applied — CSPD is education service provider, not software development company; dimension not applicable.
Basic-to-intermediate analytics inferred from Uplift data requirements and dedicated Data Intelligence EGM; no modern data warehouse confirmed.
Intermediate classification weakly inferred from government compliance obligations; lacks direct evidence of controls, SOC, or penetration testing.
Heavy burden comprehensively documented across NESA, Education Act, Schools Funding, Privacy Act, Working with Children, and Catholic governance.
Single-region scope precisely defined as Diocese of Parramatta boundary (Western Sydney and Blue Mountains); no international operations.
Recession-resistant confirmed by government funding non-cyclicality, essential service status, and Western Sydney population growth tailwind.
Nonprofit structure documented via company limited by guarantee (2023), Catholic Church canonical authority, and government accountability.
Pragmatic/evidence-driven demonstrated through Uplift framework grounded in Science of Reading and cognitive load theory; minor adoption barrier gap.
Community/mission-driven positioning confirmed through Catholic identity, student flourishing messaging, and Project Compassion engagement.
Mission-driven values confirmed through faith formation as Strategic Pillar 1 with non-negotiable mission constraints on decisions.
Strong-in-niche inferred from Teacher Scholarship Program, professional development, and active recruitment; lacks public sentiment validation.
